mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for deploying large language models efficiently and reliably, but requires careful resource management and configuration to maximize performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface to manage deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> Install the CLI binary for Linux.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new GKE cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to interact with your cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This enables autoscaling based on resource usage.
> This sets up monitoring for your deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment llm-d --all

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This deletes the llm-d deployment.
> This deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This process includes draining nodes, upgrading, and verifying the deployment.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/llm-d-v0.6.0.yaml

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to drain.
> This applies the new version of the deployment.
> Verify that all pods are running and ready.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the application logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource limits or configurations.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'NodeNotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more nodes are not in a ready state due to resource exhaustion or network issues.

**Fix:**
Check node status with 'kubectl describe nodes' and resolve any underlying issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'FailedScheduling'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There are insufficient resources available to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your cluster has enough resources and consider scaling up the node count.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new inference scheduling algorithms and improved autoscaling capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old configuration format for model deployment.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your deployment configurations to the new format as specified in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in 'Running' status and 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
